title: Determination of the lipid composition of the GPI anchor
author: Auxiliadora Aguilera-Romero †
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvban2ie

steps.instruction: Transform the yeast strain with a centromeric plasmid expressing GFP-tagged Gas1p under control of its own promoter (pRS416-GAS1-GFP).
Pick up a colony and streak it on a SD agar plate with appropriate nutritional supplements (SC-URA) and incubate them at 24 °C for 2-3 days.
Inoculate transformed yeast cells in 3ml of SC-URA in a 12 ml sterile tube and growth them to the early-to-mid logarithmic phase at 24 °C with shaking at 250 rpm.
Wash PVDF membrane strips slowly avoiding touching the membrane with 1ml of methanol (MS quality) four times.
Wash PVDF strips with 1ml of water (MS quality).
Incubate with a filtered mixture of 500 μl of 0.3 M NaOAc pH 4.0 buffer and 500 μl of freshly dissolved 1 M NaNO 2 for 3 h at 37 °C.
Remove the solution and wash the PVDF membrane strips with 1 ml of water MS-LC grade four times.
Extract the GPI-lipids from the PVDF strip with 75 μl of negative mode solvent (Chloroform/Methanol (1:2) + 5 mM Ammonium acetate).
Transfer the sample into a 96 well plate and seal with an Easy Pierce 20 µm Foil to avoid evaporation of the sample.
Infuse the sample on a TSQ Vantage using a Triversa Nanomate equipped with a HD_A chip with a gas pressure of 30 psi and a spray voltage of 1.2 kV.
Operate the TSQ Vantage with a spray voltage of 3 kV in negative-ion mode. The capillary temperature is set to 190 °C.
Use Multiple Reaction Monitoring (MRM) to obtain the signal intensity for each lipid molecular species (Fragmentation is performed with a collision energy optimized for each lipid class)

Read 3 times the MRM list (Table 1) on the TSQ Vantage to obtain three technical replicates for each biological replicate of the strain (It is advisable, to include at least, 3 biological replicates per strain) The MRM measurements are done with a width set to 1.0 in the Q3.
Calculate the signal intensity for each parent ion adding the intensities obtained in a range of ± 0.5 the m/z of the production. This can be done manually with Thermo Xcalibur software.
Finally, calculate the proportional signal for each lipid species compared to the sum of all the signals.

Dilute yeast cells into 800 ml of YPD medium and growth them to mid-log phase overnight at 30°C with shaking at 250 rpm. YPD medium is required for the correct expression of Gas1-GFP at the cell surface.

On the next day, harvest 600 x 10 7 cells by centrifugation at 3000 x g for 5 min, discard the supernatant, resuspend in 12 ml of TNE precooled at 4°C and distribute aliquots of 1 ml into 2 ml screw-cap tubes (12 tubes).
Centrifuge aliquots at 13,000 x g for 1 min, discard supernatant and freeze the pellet at -80 °C.
Break cells using Fast Prep (3 pulses of 30 s at 5 m/s).
Remove glass beads and cell debris by centrifugation at 1000 x g for 10 min at 4 °C.
Transfer 1 ml of supernatant into a 1.5 ml ultraclear tube (UC).
Centrifuge at 17,000 x g for 20 min at 4 °C.
Resuspend and combine all the pellets into a single 1.5 ml UC tube using 1 ml of TNE.
Add 250µl of TNE-D 5 % to obtain a solution with a final concentration of 1% digitonin. Mix with end-over-end rotation for 1 h at 4 °C.
Centrifuge the suspension at 17,000 x g for 20 min at 4 °C to remove insoluble material.
Transfer the supernatant to an UC tube with 100 µl GFP-Trap beads at (30 % slurry) and incubate overnight at 4 °C.
Next day, centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove supernatant.
Resuspend the beads pellet with 500 µl of TNE-D 1 % and transfer to a new UC tube.
Wash the original UC tube with 500 µl of TNE-D 1 % and combine the beads suspension with the first one.
Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove the supernatant.
Add 750 µl TNE-D 0.2 %. Repeat step 19 and 20 twice more.
Centrifuge at 5,000 x g for 30 s at 4 °C and remove the supernatant. Centrifuge again to remove the remaining liquid and dry the beads using a white micropipette tip.
Add 40 µl of SB 2x.
Heat the samples at 95 °C for 10 min.
Vortex. Centrifuge maximum speed for 5 min at room temperature.

Load the supernatant onto a 7.5 % acrylamide gel, separate the proteins by SDS-PAGE gel electrophoresis and transfer to a PVDF membrane previously activated by methanol (Wet the PVDF membrane in 100 % methanol for a few seconds and then equilibrate the membrane in transfer buffer for a few minutes until it sinks).
After transfer, stain the PVDF membrane with Amido Black Staining Solution 2x for 1min.
Wash five times with the destaining solution for 2 min.
Cut out Gas1-GFP band with a new razor blade and transfer to an amber glass tube or screw-cap Eppendorf tube.
Add 300 µl of glass beads and 1.5 ml of TNE to each cell pellet.
Dry and transfer the membrane to an amber tube. Flush the samples with N 2 and store at -80 °C.
